title: salix hastata var. farriae
intro: Salix hastata var. farriae, also known as Farria's willow or Farria's water willow, is a variety of the Salix hastata plant, which is a type of willow tree native to North America. The medicinal properties of Salix hastata var. farriae lie in its bark and leaves, which contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it a natural anti-inflammatory and pain reliever. The health benefits of using Salix hastata var. farriae include relieving headaches, reducing fever, and soothing sore throats, as well as being used to treat menstrual cramps and other women's health issues. Herbal preparations of Salix hastata var. farriae include making a tea from its leaves and bark, which can be taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits, or applying the bark to the skin to relieve pain and inflammation.
